revision.comment: Text replacement - "#redirect[[:en:Template:PAGENAME]]↵[[Category:automatic wikipedia redirects]]↵[[Category:pages created from Interlanguage links]]↵[[ja:qq]]" to "{{subst:PAGENAME}}"
revision.text:
{{infobox deity| member_of= {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde|12=draft|12=draft}} | type = Shinto | children = | father =[[Susanoo|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] | consort = | mother = }}
Torimimi was a wife of [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]] and a member of the {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde|12=draft|12=draft}}
People with mimi in their names include {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|lt=Suga no Yatsumimi|en:draft||ja||zh}}, {{ill|Futimi no Kami|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Futimi no Kami|fr|Futemimi|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Torimimi|ja|鳥取神}}, {{ill|Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|10=zh|11=多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}, who appear in the Izumo myth of the [[Kojiki]]. {{ill|Amenohibara Oshinomi no Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|12=draft|12=draft}} is also considered a regional chief
==Overview==
She is a {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Kunitsukami|en|4=fr}} that appears only in [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]] . She is the daughter of {{ill|Yashimamochinogami|ja|八島牟遅能神|1=Yashimamuji|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami|12=draft|12=draft}} and the sixth wife of [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Mikoto]] . In addition to Tottori-no-kami, the name is also written as '''Torimimi (鳥耳神)''' and '''Torikai no Kaim (鳥甘神)'''.<ref>『古事記伝』による。須賀之八耳は[[須賀郷]]ないしは[[須佐郷]]、[[美呂浪神]]は[[美呂郷]]、[[布忍富鳥鳴海神]]は[[布師郷]]のミ（首長）を表す。</ref>
“'''Mimi'''" and "'''Mi'''" are Japanese words that can be found in many names related to ancient Japan. They are very common in names of royal family members.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=魏志倭人伝、彌彌、彌彌那利|url=https://tokyox.sakura.ne.jp/forum/discussion/49/%E9%AD%8F%E5%BF%97%E5%80%AD%E4%BA%BA%E4%BC%9D-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C%E9%82%A3%E5%88%A9|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230126011011/https://tokyox.sakura.ne.jp/forum/discussion/49/%E9%AD%8F%E5%BF%97%E5%80%AD%E4%BA%BA%E4%BC%9D-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C%E9%82%A3%E5%88%A9|archive-date=2023-01-26|access-date=2023-01-26|website=Forum_tokyoblog}}</ref> They're also found in names of leaders, people, and objects. "Mi" can mean "divine spirit" and can be a part of names for deities. For example, the terms "[[Watatsumi]]" and "{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|en}}" mean "sea spirit" and "mountain spirit" respectively and both use the version 霊.<ref name=":1">[[本居宣長]]『[[古事記伝]]』七之巻。</ref>
Spellings include {{nihongo|Mimi|ミミ、耳、彌彌、美美|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{Nihongo|MI|彌、見、美、海、看}}.
In ancient Japanese historical texts like "[[Kojiki]]" and "[[Nihonshoki]]", people with "Mi" or "Mimi" in their names were often leaders in their regions. For instance, "{{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|en}}" ruled in the [[Izumi Province|Izumi]] and [[Settsu Province|Settsu regions]], "Kugamimi" ruled in the [[Tamba Province|Tamba region]], and "Masakitsumimi" ruled in the [[Tajima Province|Tajima region]].<ref name=":2">溝口睦子「記紀神話解釈の一つのこころみ」『文学』1973rd year - 1974th year 。波多都美は[[Izumo Province ]][[飯石郡]][[波多郷]]の首長、支自麻都美はIzumo Province 飯石郡[[来島郷]]の首長である。</ref> and minor chiefs named Hatatsumi and Kijimatsumi are mentioned.<ref name=":2" />
In ancient Japan, birds were believed to carry human souls, and catching them was a religious ritual, so the name is thought to mean "catching birds" <ref>[[西宮一民]]「付録 神名の釈義」『新潮日本古典集成 古事記』[[新潮社]]、2014th year 、381頁。</ref> .
==Other boilerplate== {{ill| Tottori-no-Kami|ja}} is one of the {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde|12=draft|12=draft}}. This is a group of Japanese deities ({{ill|Kunitsukami|en}}) that are all in a genealogical relationship descending from {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== Overview of the Generations == {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}} married {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|12=draft|12=draft}} (daughter of [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi]]) and had {{ill|Yashimajinumi|ja|八島士奴美神|1=Yashimajinumi|en|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="auto">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Yashimajinumi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=181|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>.
This god married {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Konohanachiru-hime|ja|木花知流比売|en}}<ref name="auto" /> (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|12=draft|12=draft}}) and had {{ill|Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr|12=draft|12=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en|12=draft|12=draft}}) and had {{ill|Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|1=Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|1=Ame-no-Tsudoechine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami|12=draft|12=draft}} and had {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|1=Omizunu|simple|User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|en|12=draft|12=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|12=draft|12=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Funodomuno Kami|ja|布怒豆怒神|1=Funozuno|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funodomuno Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}) and had {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|1=Ame-no-Fuyukinu|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|12=draft|12=draft}}) and had {{ill|Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref>{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr|12=draft|12=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en|12=draft|12=draft}}) and had {{ill|Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|1=Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Ame-no-Tsudoechine|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|1=Ame-no-Tsudoechine|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine|12=draft|12=draft}} and had {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|1=Omizunu|simple|User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|en|12=draft|12=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|12=draft|12=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|1=Funozuno|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|12=draft|12=draft}}) and had {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|1=Ame-no-Fuyukinu|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (This Amanofuyuorihime Kami is also known as Amanofukine Kami{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}, and is said to be the child of Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。|12=draft|12=draft}}. The child {{ill|Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|ja|清武豊彦命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}} is the ancestor of the {{ill|Ono Family (Shake)|lt=Ono Family|ja|小野氏 (社家)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ono Family (Shake)|lt=Ono Family|12=draft|12=draft}} who were priests of {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。|12=draft|12=draft}}{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}.)
This god married {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|1=Sashikuni Wakahime|2=ja|3=刺国若比売|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|12=draft|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|12=draft|12=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Ōkami|fr|12=draft|12=draft}}) and had {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi no Kami|12=draft|12=draft}} (Ōnamuchi no Kami. Half-sibling to the {{ill|Yasogami|ja|八十神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|12=draft|12=draft}}).
This god married {{ill|Tottori Kami|ja|鳥取神|1=Tottori-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tottori Kami|12=draft|12=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Yashimamochinogami|ja|八島牟遅能神|1=Yashimamuji|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami|12=draft|12=draft}}) and had {{ill|Torinarumi Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|12=draft|12=draft}} and had {{ill|Kunishirotomi Kami|ja|国忍富神|1=Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|1=Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kunishirotomi Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Ashinataka-no-Kami|ja|葦那陀迦神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka-no-Kami|12=draft|12=draft}} and had {{ill|Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|ja|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|1=Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Sakitamahime|ja|前玉比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sakitamahime|12=draft|12=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanomikemunushi no Kami|ja|天之甕主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanomikemunushi no Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}) and had {{ill|Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|ja|甕主日子神|1=Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime|12=draft|12=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en|12=draft|12=draft}}) and had {{ill|Tahirikishimarumi Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|10=zh|11=多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tahirikishimarumi Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|12=draft|12=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|ja|比比羅木之其花麻豆美神|1=Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}) and had {{ill|Mironami Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Aonuma-Mashi-no-Maou-hime|ja|青沼馬沼押比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Aonuma-Mashi-no-Maou-hime|12=draft|12=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Shikiyamanushi no Kami|ja|敷山主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shikiyamanushi no Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}) and had {{ill|Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|ja|布忍富鳥鳴海神|1=Nunobutomi-Torinarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Wakatsukime Kami|ja|若尽女神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Wakatsukime Kami|12=draft|12=draft}} and had {{ill|Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|1=Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Totsumatone Kami|ja|遠津待根神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsumatone Kami|12=draft|12=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanosagiri Kami|ja|天之狭霧神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanosagiri Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}) and had {{ill|Totsuyami-Sakitara-no-Kami|ja|遠津山岬多良斯神|1=Totsuyami-Sakitara-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsuyami-Sakitara-no-Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}.

{{infobox deity| member_of= {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde|12=draft|12=draft}} | type = Shinto | children = | father =[[Susanoo|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] | consort = | mother = }}
Torimimi was a wife of [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]] and a member of the {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde|12=draft|12=draft}}
People with mimi in their names include {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|lt=Suga no Yatsumimi|en:draft||ja||zh}}, {{ill|Futimi no Kami|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Futimi no Kami|fr|Futemimi|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Torimimi|ja|鳥取神}}, {{ill|Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|10=zh|11=多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}, who appear in the Izumo myth of the [[Kojiki]]. {{ill|Amenohibara Oshinomi no Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|12=draft|12=draft}} is also considered a regional chief
==Overview==
She is a {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Kunitsukami|en|4=fr}} that appears only in [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]] . She is the daughter of {{ill|Yashimamochinogami|ja|八島牟遅能神|1=Yashimamuji|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami|12=draft|12=draft}} and the sixth wife of [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Mikoto]] . In addition to Tottori-no-kami, the name is also written as '''Torimimi (鳥耳神)''' and '''Torikai no Kaim (鳥甘神)'''.<ref>『古事記伝』による。須賀之八耳は[[須賀郷]]ないしは[[須佐郷]]、[[美呂浪神]]は[[美呂郷]]、[[布忍富鳥鳴海神]]は[[布師郷]]のミ（首長）を表す。</ref>
“'''Mimi'''" and "'''Mi'''" are Japanese words that can be found in many names related to ancient Japan. They are very common in names of royal family members.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=魏志倭人伝、彌彌、彌彌那利|url=https://tokyox.sakura.ne.jp/forum/discussion/49/%E9%AD%8F%E5%BF%97%E5%80%AD%E4%BA%BA%E4%BC%9D-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C%E9%82%A3%E5%88%A9|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230126011011/https://tokyox.sakura.ne.jp/forum/discussion/49/%E9%AD%8F%E5%BF%97%E5%80%AD%E4%BA%BA%E4%BC%9D-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C%E9%82%A3%E5%88%A9|archive-date=2023-01-26|access-date=2023-01-26|website=Forum_tokyoblog}}</ref> They're also found in names of leaders, people, and objects. "Mi" can mean "divine spirit" and can be a part of names for deities. For example, the terms "[[Watatsumi]]" and "{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|en}}" mean "sea spirit" and "mountain spirit" respectively and both use the version 霊.<ref name=":1">[[本居宣長]]『[[古事記伝]]』七之巻。</ref>
Spellings include {{nihongo|Mimi|ミミ、耳、彌彌、美美|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{Nihongo|MI|彌、見、美、海、看}}.
In ancient Japanese historical texts like "[[Kojiki]]" and "[[Nihonshoki]]", people with "Mi" or "Mimi" in their names were often leaders in their regions. For instance, "{{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|en}}" ruled in the [[Izumi Province|Izumi]] and [[Settsu Province|Settsu regions]], "Kugamimi" ruled in the [[Tamba Province|Tamba region]], and "Masakitsumimi" ruled in the [[Tajima Province|Tajima region]].<ref name=":2">溝口睦子「記紀神話解釈の一つのこころみ」『文学』1973rd year - 1974th year 。波多都美は[[Izumo Province ]][[飯石郡]][[波多郷]]の首長、支自麻都美はIzumo Province 飯石郡[[来島郷]]の首長である。</ref> and minor chiefs named Hatatsumi and Kijimatsumi are mentioned.<ref name=":2" />
In ancient Japan, birds were believed to carry human souls, and catching them was a religious ritual, so the name is thought to mean "catching birds" <ref>[[西宮一民]]「付録 神名の釈義」『新潮日本古典集成 古事記』[[新潮社]]、2014th year 、381頁。</ref> .
==Other boilerplate== {{ill|Tottori-no-Kami|ja}} is one of the {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde|12=draft|12=draft}}. This is a group of Japanese deities ({{ill|Kunitsukami|en}}) that are all in a genealogical relationship descending from {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== Overview of the Generations == {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}} married {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|12=draft|12=draft}} (daughter of [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi]]) and had {{ill|Yashimajinumi|ja|八島士奴美神|1=Yashimajinumi|en|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="auto">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Yashimajinumi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=181|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>.
This god married {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Konohanachiru-hime|ja|木花知流比売|en}}<ref name="auto" /> (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|12=draft|12=draft}}) and had {{ill|Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr|12=draft|12=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en|12=draft|12=draft}}) and had {{ill|Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|1=Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|1=Ame-no-Tsudoechine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami|12=draft|12=draft}} and had {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|1=Omizunu|simple|User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|en|12=draft|12=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|12=draft|12=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Funodomuno Kami|ja|布怒豆怒神|1=Funozuno|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funodomuno Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}) and had {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|1=Ame-no-Fuyukinu|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|12=draft|12=draft}}) and had {{ill|Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref>{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr|12=draft|12=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en|12=draft|12=draft}}) and had {{ill|Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|1=Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Ame-no-Tsudoechine|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|1=Ame-no-Tsudoechine|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine|12=draft|12=draft}} and had {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|1=Omizunu|simple|User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|en|12=draft|12=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|12=draft|12=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|1=Funozuno|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|12=draft|12=draft}}) and had {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|1=Ame-no-Fuyukinu|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (This Amanofuyuorihime Kami is also known as Amanofukine Kami{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}, and is said to be the child of Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。|12=draft|12=draft}}. The child {{ill|Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|ja|清武豊彦命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}} is the ancestor of the {{ill|Ono Family (Shake)|lt=Ono Family|ja|小野氏 (社家)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ono Family (Shake)|lt=Ono Family|12=draft|12=draft}} who were priests of {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。|12=draft|12=draft}}{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}.)
This god married {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|1=Sashikuni Wakahime|2=ja|3=刺国若比売|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|12=draft|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|12=draft|12=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Ōkami|fr|12=draft|12=draft}}) and had {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi no Kami|12=draft|12=draft}} (Ōnamuchi no Kami. Half-sibling to the {{ill|Yasogami|ja|八十神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|12=draft|12=draft}}).
This god married {{ill|Tottori Kami|ja|鳥取神|1=Tottori-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tottori Kami|12=draft|12=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Yashimamochinogami|ja|八島牟遅能神|1=Yashimamuji|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami|12=draft|12=draft}}) and had {{ill|Torinarumi Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|12=draft|12=draft}} and had {{ill|Kunishirotomi Kami|ja|国忍富神|1=Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|1=Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kunishirotomi Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Ashinataka-no-Kami|ja|葦那陀迦神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka-no-Kami|12=draft|12=draft}} and had {{ill|Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|ja|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|1=Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Sakitamahime|ja|前玉比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sakitamahime|12=draft|12=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanomikemunushi no Kami|ja|天之甕主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanomikemunushi no Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}) and had {{ill|Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|ja|甕主日子神|1=Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime|12=draft|12=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en|12=draft|12=draft}}) and had {{ill|Tahirikishimarumi Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|10=zh|11=多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tahirikishimarumi Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|12=draft|12=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|ja|比比羅木之其花麻豆美神|1=Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}) and had {{ill|Mironami Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Aonuma-Mashi-no-Maou-hime|ja|青沼馬沼押比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Aonuma-Mashi-no-Maou-hime|12=draft|12=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Shikiyamanushi no Kami|ja|敷山主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shikiyamanushi no Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}) and had {{ill|Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|ja|布忍富鳥鳴海神|1=Nunobutomi-Torinarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Wakatsukime Kami|ja|若尽女神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Wakatsukime Kami|12=draft|12=draft}} and had {{ill|Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|1=Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Totsumatone Kami|ja|遠津待根神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsumatone Kami|12=draft|12=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanosagiri Kami|ja|天之狭霧神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanosagiri Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}) and had {{ill|Totsuyami-Sakitara-no-Kami|ja|遠津山岬多良斯神|1=Totsuyami-Sakitara-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsuyami-Sakitara-no-Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}.

{{infobox deity| member_of= {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde|12=draft|12=draft}} | type = Shinto | children = | father =[[Susanoo|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] | consort = | mother = }}
Torimimi was a wife of [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]] and a member of the {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde|12=draft|12=draft}}
People with mimi in their names include {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|lt=Suga no Yatsumimi|en:draft||ja||zh}}, {{ill|Futimi no Kami|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Futimi no Kami|fr|Futemimi|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Torimimi|ja|鳥取神}}, {{ill|Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|10=zh|11=多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}, who appear in the Izumo myth of the [[Kojiki]]. {{ill|Amenohibara Oshinomi no Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|12=draft|12=draft}} is also considered a regional chief
==Overview==
She is a {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Kunitsukami|en|4=fr}} that appears only in [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]] . She is the daughter of {{ill|Yashimamochinogami|ja|八島牟遅能神|1=Yashimamuji|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami|12=draft|12=draft}} and the sixth wife of [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Mikoto]] . In addition to Tottori-no-kami, the name is also written as '''Torimimi (鳥耳神)''' and '''Torikai no Kaim (鳥甘神)'''.<ref>『古事記伝』による。須賀之八耳は[[須賀郷]]ないしは[[須佐郷]]、[[美呂浪神]]は[[美呂郷]]、[[布忍富鳥鳴海神]]は[[布師郷]]のミ（首長）を表す。</ref>

[[ja:鳥取神]] [[sn:Tottori-no-Kami]]
<!--Tottori-no-Kami--> <!--Tottori-no-Kami-->

{{infobox deity| member_of= {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde|12=draft|12=draft}} | type = Shinto | children = | father =[[Susanoo|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] | consort = | mother = }}
Torimimi was a wife of [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]] and a member of the {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde|12=draft|12=draft}}
People with mimi in their names include {{ill||Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|lt=Suga no Yatsumimi|en:draft||ja||zh}}, {{ill|Futimi no Kami|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Futimi no Kami|fr|Futemimi|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill||Torimimi|ja|鳥取神}}, {{ill|Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|10=zh|11=多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}, who appear in the Izumo myth of the [[Kojiki]]. {{ill|Amenohibara Oshinomi no Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|12=draft|12=draft}} is also considered a regional chief
==Overview==
She is a {{ill||Kunitsukami|en|4=fr}} that appears only in [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]] . She is the daughter of {{ill|Yashimamochinogami|ja|八島牟遅能神|1=Yashimamuji|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami|12=draft|12=draft}} and the sixth wife of [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Mikoto]] . In addition to Tottori-no-kami, the name is also written as '''Torimimi (鳥耳神)''' and '''Torikai no Kaim (鳥甘神)'''.<ref>『古事記伝』による。須賀之八耳は[[須賀郷]]ないしは[[須佐郷]]、[[美呂浪神]]は[[美呂郷]]、[[布忍富鳥鳴海神]]は[[布師郷]]のミ（首長）を表す。</ref>
“'''Mimi'''" and "'''Mi'''" are Japanese words that can be found in many names related to ancient Japan. They are very common in names of royal family members.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=魏志倭人伝、彌彌、彌彌那利|url=https://tokyox.sakura.ne.jp/forum/discussion/49/%E9%AD%8F%E5%BF%97%E5%80%AD%E4%BA%BA%E4%BC%9D-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C%E9%82%A3%E5%88%A9|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230126011011/https://tokyox.sakura.ne.jp/forum/discussion/49/%E9%AD%8F%E5%BF%97%E5%80%AD%E4%BA%BA%E4%BC%9D-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C%E9%82%A3%E5%88%A9|archive-date=2023-01-26|access-date=2023-01-26|website=Forum_tokyoblog}}</ref> They're also found in names of leaders, people, and objects. "Mi" can mean "divine spirit" and can be a part of names for deities. For example, the terms "[[Watatsumi]]" and "{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|en}}" mean "sea spirit" and "mountain spirit" respectively and both use the version 霊.<ref name=":1">[[本居宣長]]『[[古事記伝]]』七之巻。</ref>
Spellings include {{nihongo|Mimi|ミミ、耳、彌彌、美美|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{Nihongo|MI|彌、見、美、海、看}}.
In ancient Japanese historical texts like "[[Kojiki]]" and "[[Nihonshoki]]", people with "Mi" or "Mimi" in their names were often leaders in their regions. For instance, "{{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|en}}" ruled in the [[Izumi Province|Izumi]] and [[Settsu Province|Settsu regions]], "Kugamimi" ruled in the [[Tamba Province|Tamba region]], and "Masakitsumimi" ruled in the [[Tajima Province|Tajima region]].<ref name=":2">溝口睦子「記紀神話解釈の一つのこころみ」『文学』1973rd year - 1974th year 。波多都美は[[Izumo Province ]][[飯石郡]][[波多郷]]の首長、支自麻都美はIzumo Province 飯石郡[[来島郷]]の首長である。</ref> and minor chiefs named Hatatsumi and Kijimatsumi are mentioned.<ref name=":2" />
In ancient Japan, birds were believed to carry human souls, and catching them was a religious ritual, so the name is thought to mean "catching birds" <ref>[[西宮一民]]「付録 神名の釈義」『新潮日本古典集成 古事記』[[新潮社]]、2014th year 、381頁。</ref> .
==Other boilerplate== {{ill|Tottori-no-Kami|ja}} is one of the {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde|12=draft|12=draft}}. This is a group of Japanese deities ({{ill|Kunitsukami|en}}) that are all in a genealogical relationship descending from {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}}.

This god married {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime|12=draft|12=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en|12=draft|12=draft}}) and had {{ill|Tahirikishimarumi Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|10=zh|11=多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tahirikishimarumi Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|12=draft|12=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|ja|比比羅木之其花麻豆美神|1=Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}) and had {{ill|Mironami Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Aonuma-Mashi-no-Maou-hime|ja|青沼馬沼押比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Aonuma-Mashi-no-Maou-hime|12=draft|12=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Shikiyamanushi no Kami|ja|敷山主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shikiyamanushi no Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}) and had {{ill|Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|ja|布忍富鳥鳴海神|1=Nunobutomi-Torinarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Wakatsukime Kami|ja|若尽女神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Wakatsukime Kami|12=draft|12=draft}} and had {{ill|Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|1=Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Totsumatone Kami|ja|遠津待根神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsumatone Kami|12=draft|12=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanosagiri Kami|ja|天之狭霧神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanosagiri Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}) and had {{ill|Totsuyami-Sakitara-no-Kami|ja|遠津山岬多良斯神|1=Totsuyami-Sakitara-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsuyami-Sakitara-no-Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}.

[[ja:鳥取神]] [[sn:Tottori-no-Kami]]
<!--Tottori-no-Kami--> <!--Tottori-no-Kami-->

{{infobox deity| member_of= {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde|12=draft|12=draft}} | type = Shinto | children = | father =[[Susanoo|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] | consort = | mother = }}
Torimimi was a wife of [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]] and a member of the {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde|12=draft|12=draft}}
People with mimi in their names include {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|lt=Suga no Yatsumimi|en:draft||ja||zh}}, {{ill|Futimi no Kami|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Futimi no Kami|fr|Futemimi|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Torimimi|ja|鳥取神}}, {{ill|Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|10=zh|11=多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}, who appear in the Izumo myth of the [[Kojiki]]. {{ill|Amenohibara Oshinomi no Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|12=draft|12=draft}} is also considered a regional chief
==Overview==
She is a {{ill|Kunitsukami|en|4=fr}} that appears only in [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]] . She is the daughter of {{ill|Yashimamochinogami|ja|八島牟遅能神|1=Yashimamuji|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami|12=draft|12=draft}} and the sixth wife of [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Mikoto]] . In addition to Tottori-no-kami, the name is also written as '''Torimimi (鳥耳神)''' and '''Torikai no Kaim (鳥甘神)'''.<ref>『古事記伝』による。須賀之八耳は[[須賀郷]]ないしは[[須佐郷]]、[[美呂浪神]]は[[美呂郷]]、[[布忍富鳥鳴海神]]は[[布師郷]]のミ（首長）を表す。</ref>
“'''Mimi'''" and "'''Mi'''" are Japanese words that can be found in many names related to ancient Japan. They are very common in names of royal family members.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=魏志倭人伝、彌彌、彌彌那利|url=https://tokyox.sakura.ne.jp/forum/discussion/49/%E9%AD%8F%E5%BF%97%E5%80%AD%E4%BA%BA%E4%BC%9D-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C%E9%82%A3%E5%88%A9|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230126011011/https://tokyox.sakura.ne.jp/forum/discussion/49/%E9%AD%8F%E5%BF%97%E5%80%AD%E4%BA%BA%E4%BC%9D-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C%E9%82%A3%E5%88%A9|archive-date=2023-01-26|access-date=2023-01-26|website=Forum_tokyoblog}}</ref> They're also found in names of leaders, people, and objects. "Mi" can mean "divine spirit" and can be a part of names for deities. For example, the terms "[[Watatsumi]]" and "{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|en}}" mean "sea spirit" and "mountain spirit" respectively and both use the version 霊.<ref name=":1">[[本居宣長]]『[[古事記伝]]』七之巻。</ref>
Spellings include {{nihongo|Mimi|ミミ、耳、彌彌、美美|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{Nihongo|MI|彌、見、美、海、看}}.
In ancient Japanese historical texts like "[[Kojiki]]" and "[[Nihonshoki]]", people with "Mi" or "Mimi" in their names were often leaders in their regions. For instance, "{{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|en}}" ruled in the [[Izumi Province|Izumi]] and [[Settsu Province|Settsu regions]], "Kugamimi" ruled in the [[Tamba Province|Tamba region]], and "Masakitsumimi" ruled in the [[Tajima Province|Tajima region]].<ref name=":2">溝口睦子「記紀神話解釈の一つのこころみ」『文学』1973rd year - 1974th year 。波多都美は[[Izumo Province ]][[飯石郡]][[波多郷]]の首長、支自麻都美はIzumo Province 飯石郡[[来島郷]]の首長である。</ref> and minor chiefs named Hatatsumi and Kijimatsumi are mentioned.<ref name=":2" />
In ancient Japan, birds were believed to carry human souls, and catching them was a religious ritual, so the name is thought to mean "catching birds" <ref>[[西宮一民]]「付録 神名の釈義」『新潮日本古典集成 古事記』[[新潮社]]、2014th year 、381頁。</ref> .
==Other boilerplate== {{ill|Tottori-no-Kami|ja}} is one of the {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde|12=draft|12=draft}}. This is a group of Japanese deities ({{ill|Kunitsukami|en}}) that are all in a genealogical relationship descending from {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== Overview of the Generations == {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}} married {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|12=draft|12=draft}} (daughter of [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi]]) and had {{ill|Yashimajinumi|ja|八島士奴美神|1=Yashimajinumi|en|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="auto">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Yashimajinumi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=181|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>.
This god married {{ill|Konohanachiru-hime|ja|木花知流比売|en}}<ref name="auto" /> (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|12=draft|12=draft}}) and had {{ill|Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr|12=draft|12=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en|12=draft|12=draft}}) and had {{ill|Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|1=Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|1=Ame-no-Tsudoechine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami|12=draft|12=draft}} and had {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|1=Omizunu|simple|User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|en|12=draft|12=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|12=draft|12=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Funodomuno Kami|ja|布怒豆怒神|1=Funozuno|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funodomuno Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}) and had {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|1=Ame-no-Fuyukinu|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|12=draft|12=draft}}) and had {{ill|Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref>{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr|12=draft|12=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en|12=draft|12=draft}}) and had {{ill|Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|1=Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Ame-no-Tsudoechine|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|1=Ame-no-Tsudoechine|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine|12=draft|12=draft}} and had {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|1=Omizunu|simple|User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|en|12=draft|12=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|12=draft|12=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|1=Funozuno|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|12=draft|12=draft}}) and had {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|1=Ame-no-Fuyukinu|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (This Amanofuyuorihime Kami is also known as Amanofukine Kami{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}, and is said to be the child of Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。|12=draft|12=draft}}. The child {{ill|Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|ja|清武豊彦命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}} is the ancestor of the {{ill|Ono Family (Shake)|lt=Ono Family|ja|小野氏 (社家)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ono Family (Shake)|lt=Ono Family|12=draft|12=draft}} who were priests of {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。|12=draft|12=draft}}{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}.)
This god married {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|1=Sashikuni Wakahime|2=ja|3=刺国若比売|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|12=draft|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|12=draft|12=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Ōkami|fr|12=draft|12=draft}}) and had {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi no Kami|12=draft|12=draft}} (Ōnamuchi no Kami. Half-sibling to the {{ill|Yasogami|ja|八十神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|12=draft|12=draft}}).
This god married {{ill|Tottori Kami|ja|鳥取神|1=Tottori-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tottori Kami|12=draft|12=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Yashimamochinogami|ja|八島牟遅能神|1=Yashimamuji|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami|12=draft|12=draft}}) and had {{ill|Torinarumi Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}.

This god married {{ill|Sakitamahime|ja|前玉比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sakitamahime|12=draft|12=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanomikemunushi no Kami|ja|天之甕主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanomikemunushi no Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}) and had {{ill|Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|ja|甕主日子神|1=Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime|12=draft|12=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en|12=draft|12=draft}}) and had {{ill|Tahirikishimarumi Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|10=zh|11=多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tahirikishimarumi Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}.

She is a {{ill|Kunitsukami|en|4=fr}} that appears only in [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]] . She is the daughter of {{ill|Yashimamochinogami|ja|八島牟遅能神|1=Yashimamuji|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the sixth wife of [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Mikoto]] . In addition to Tottori-no-kami, the name is also written as '''Torimimi (鳥耳神)''' and '''Torikai no Kaim (鳥甘神)'''.<ref>『古事記伝』による。須賀之八耳は[[須賀郷]]ないしは[[須佐郷]]、[[美呂浪神]]は[[美呂郷]]、[[布忍富鳥鳴海神]]は[[布師郷]]のミ（首長）を表す。</ref>
“'''Mimi'''" and "'''Mi'''" are Japanese words that can be found in many names related to ancient Japan. They are very common in names of royal family members.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=魏志倭人伝、彌彌、彌彌那利|url=https://tokyox.sakura.ne.jp/forum/discussion/49/%E9%AD%8F%E5%BF%97%E5%80%AD%E4%BA%BA%E4%BC%9D-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C%E9%82%A3%E5%88%A9|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230126011011/https://tokyox.sakura.ne.jp/forum/discussion/49/%E9%AD%8F%E5%BF%97%E5%80%AD%E4%BA%BA%E4%BC%9D-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C%E9%82%A3%E5%88%A9|archive-date=2023-01-26|access-date=2023-01-26|website=Forum_tokyoblog}}</ref> They're also found in names of leaders, people, and objects. "Mi" can mean "divine spirit" and can be a part of names for deities. For example, the terms "[[Watatsumi]]" and "{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|en}}" mean "sea spirit" and "mountain spirit" respectively and both use the version 霊.<ref name=":1">[[本居宣長]]『[[古事記伝]]』七之巻。</ref>
Spellings include {{nihongo|Mimi|ミミ、耳、彌彌、美美|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{Nihongo|MI|彌、見、美、海、看}}.
In ancient Japanese historical texts like "[[Kojiki]]" and "[[Nihonshoki]]", people with "Mi" or "Mimi" in their names were often leaders in their regions. For instance, "{{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|en}}" ruled in the [[Izumi Province|Izumi]] and [[Settsu Province|Settsu regions]], "Kugamimi" ruled in the [[Tamba Province|Tamba region]], and "Masakitsumimi" ruled in the [[Tajima Province|Tajima region]].<ref name=":2">溝口睦子「記紀神話解釈の一つのこころみ」『文学』1973rd year - 1974th year 。波多都美は[[Izumo Province ]][[飯石郡]][[波多郷]]の首長、支自麻都美はIzumo Province 飯石郡[[来島郷]]の首長である。</ref> and minor chiefs named Hatatsumi and Kijimatsumi are mentioned.<ref name=":2" />
In ancient Japan, birds were believed to carry human souls, and catching them was a religious ritual, so the name is thought to mean "catching birds" <ref>[[西宮一民]]「付録 神名の釈義」『新潮日本古典集成 古事記』[[新潮社]]、2014th year 、381頁。</ref> .
==Other boilerplate== {{ill|Tottori-no-Kami|ja}} is one of the {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. This is a group of Japanese deities ({{ill|Kunitsukami|en}}) that are all in a genealogical relationship descending from {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Overview of the Generations == {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}} married {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (daughter of [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi]]) and had {{ill|Yashimajinumi|ja|八島士奴美神|1=Yashimajinumi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="auto">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Yashimajinumi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=181|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>.
This god married {{ill|Konohanachiru-hime|ja|木花知流比売|en}}<ref name="auto" /> (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and had {{ill|Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and had {{ill|Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|1=Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|1=Ame-no-Tsudoechine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and had {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|1=Omizunu|simple|User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Funodomuno Kami|ja|布怒豆怒神|1=Funozuno|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funodomuno Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and had {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|1=Ame-no-Fuyukinu|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and had {{ill|Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref>{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and had {{ill|Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|1=Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Ame-no-Tsudoechine|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|1=Ame-no-Tsudoechine|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and had {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|1=Omizunu|simple|User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|1=Funozuno|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and had {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|1=Ame-no-Fuyukinu|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (This Amanofuyuorihime Kami is also known as Amanofukine Kami{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}, and is said to be the child of Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The child {{ill|Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|ja|清武豊彦命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is the ancestor of the {{ill|Ono Family (Shake)|lt=Ono Family|ja|小野氏 (社家)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ono Family (Shake)|lt=Ono Family|qq=draft|qq=draft}} who were priests of {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}.)
This god married {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|1=Sashikuni Wakahime|2=ja|3=刺国若比売|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=draft|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Ōkami|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and had {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Ōnamuchi no Kami. Half-sibling to the {{ill|Yasogami|ja|八十神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}).
This god married {{ill|Tottori Kami|ja|鳥取神|1=Tottori-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tottori Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Yashimamochinogami|ja|八島牟遅能神|1=Yashimamuji|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and had {{ill|Torinarumi Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and had {{ill|Kunishirotomi Kami|ja|国忍富神|1=Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|1=Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kunishirotomi Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Ashinataka-no-Kami|ja|葦那陀迦神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and had {{ill|Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|ja|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|1=Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Sakitamahime|ja|前玉比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sakitamahime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanomikemunushi no Kami|ja|天之甕主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanomikemunushi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and had {{ill|Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|ja|甕主日子神|1=Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and had {{ill|Tahirikishimarumi Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|10=zh|11=多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tahirikishimarumi Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|ja|比比羅木之其花麻豆美神|1=Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and had {{ill|Mironami Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Aonuma-Mashi-no-Maou-hime|ja|青沼馬沼押比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Aonuma-Mashi-no-Maou-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Shikiyamanushi no Kami|ja|敷山主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shikiyamanushi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and had {{ill|Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|ja|布忍富鳥鳴海神|1=Nunobutomi-Torinarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Wakatsukime Kami|ja|若尽女神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Wakatsukime Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and had {{ill|Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|1=Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Totsumatone Kami|ja|遠津待根神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsumatone Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanosagiri Kami|ja|天之狭霧神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanosagiri Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and had {{ill|Totsuyami-Sakitara-no-Kami|ja|遠津山岬多良斯神|1=Totsuyami-Sakitara-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsuyami-Sakitara-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.

revision.comment: 33 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:鳥取神 up to rev 101649062
revision.text:
{{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 = 鳥取神 | 世代名 = | 先代= | 次代= | 神祇 = <!-- 天神、地祇、天津神、国津神など -->[[国津神]] | 全名 = 鳥取神（トトリノカミ） | 別名 = 鳥耳神、鳥甘神 | 別称 = | 神階 = | 神格 = <!-- 太陽、月、山、海など司るもの -->神事の神 | 陵所 = | 父 = [[八島牟遅能神]] | 母 = | 親 = <!-- まぐあいによって生まれない場合 --> | 配偶者 = [[大国主神]] | 子 = [[鳥鳴海神]] | 宮 = | 神社 = | 記紀等 = [[古事記]] }} '''鳥取神'''（トトリ）は[[日本神話]]に登場する[[神 (神道)|女神]]。'''鳥耳神'''（トリミミ）、'''鳥甘神'''（トリカイ）ともする。 == 概要 == 『古事記』にのみ登場する[[国津神]]。八島牟遅能神の娘であり、大国主神の六番目の妻。名称は鳥取神の他に、'''鳥耳神'''（トリミミノカミ）、'''鳥甘神'''（トリカイノカミ）などとも表記する。
古代日本において鳥は人間の霊魂を運ぶと考えられ、その鳥を捕まえることは神事であり、名義は「鳥を捕まえること」と考えられる{{Sfn|西宮|2014|p=381}}。 == 系譜 == [[画像:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|大国主の系図（『古事記』による）。青は男神、赤は女神、黄は性別不詳]] 八島牟遅能神の娘で、大国主神との間に鳥鳴海神をもうけた。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * <!-- にしみや -->{{Cite book|和書 |title=古事記 |volume= |date=2014年10月30日 |publisher=[[新潮社]] |ref={{SfnRef|西宮|2014}} |author=[[西宮一民]] 校注 |isbn=978-4-10-620801-0 |series=[[新潮日本古典集成]]（新装版）}} == 関連項目 == * [[十七世神]] * [[鳥取部]] == 外部リンク == * [https://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/totorinokami/ 鳥取神 – 國學院大學 古典文化学事業] {{日本神話}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:ととりのかみ}} [[Category:国津神]] [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:女神]] [[Category:古代日本の人物]] [[Category:古墳時代の人物]] [[Category:長門国の人物]] [[Category:国造]] [[Category:十七世神]]

古代日本において鳥は人間の霊魂を運ぶと考えられ、その鳥を捕まえることは神事であり、名義は「鳥を捕まえること」と考えられる{{Sfn|西宮|2014|p=381}}。 == 系譜 == [[画像:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|大国主の系図（『古事記』による）。青は男神、赤は女神、黄は性別不詳]] 八島牟遅能神の娘で、大国主神との間に鳥鳴海神をもうけた。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * <!-- にしみや -->{{Cite book|和書 |title=古事記 |volume= |date=2014年10月30日 |publisher=[[新潮社]] |ref={{SfnRef|西宮|2014}} |author=[[西宮一民]] 校注 |isbn=978-4-10-620801-0 |series=[[新潮日本古典集成]]（新装版）}} == 関連項目 == * [[十七世神]] * [[鳥取部]] == 外部リンク == * [https://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/totorinokami/ 鳥取神 – 國學院大學 古典文化学事業] {{日本神話}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:ととりのかみ}} [[Category:国津神]] [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:女神]] [[Category:古代日本の人物]] [[Category:古墳時代の人物]] [[Category:長門国の人物]] [[Category:国造]] [[Category:十七世神]]

{{infobox deity| member_of= {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | type = Shinto | children = | father =[[Susanoo|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] | consort = | mother = }}
Torimimi was a wife of [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]] and a member of the {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
People with mimi in their names include {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|lt=Suga no Yatsumimi|en:draft||ja||zh}}, {{ill|Futimi no Kami|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Futimi no Kami|fr|Futemimi|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Torimimi|ja|鳥取神}}, {{ill|Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|10=zh|11=多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who appear in the Izumo myth of the [[Kojiki]]. {{ill|Amenohibara Oshinomi no Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is also considered a regional chief
==Overview==
She is a {{ill|Kunitsukami|en|4=fr}} that appears only in [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]] . She is the daughter of {{ill|Yashimamochinogami|ja|八島牟遅能神|1=Yashimamuji|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the sixth wife of [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Mikoto]] . In addition to Tottori-no-kami, the name is also written as '''Torimimi (鳥耳神)''' and '''Torikai no Kaim (鳥甘神)'''.<ref>『古事記伝』による。須賀之八耳は[[須賀郷]]ないしは[[須佐郷]]、[[美呂浪神]]は[[美呂郷]]、[[布忍富鳥鳴海神]]は[[布師郷]]のミ（首長）を表す。</ref>
“'''Mimi'''" and "'''Mi'''" are Japanese words that can be found in many names related to ancient Japan. They are very common in names of royal family members.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=魏志倭人伝、彌彌、彌彌那利|url=https://tokyox.sakura.ne.jp/forum/discussion/49/%E9%AD%8F%E5%BF%97%E5%80%AD%E4%BA%BA%E4%BC%9D-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C%E9%82%A3%E5%88%A9|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230126011011/https://tokyox.sakura.ne.jp/forum/discussion/49/%E9%AD%8F%E5%BF%97%E5%80%AD%E4%BA%BA%E4%BC%9D-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C%E9%82%A3%E5%88%A9|archive-date=2023-01-26|access-date=2023-01-26|website=Forum_tokyoblog}}</ref> They're also found in names of leaders, people, and objects. "Mi" can mean "divine spirit" and can be a part of names for deities. For example, the terms "[[Watatsumi]]" and "{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|en}}" mean "sea spirit" and "mountain spirit" respectively and both use the version 霊.<ref name=":1">[[本居宣長]]『[[古事記伝]]』七之巻。</ref>
Spellings include {{nihongo|Mimi|ミミ、耳、彌彌、美美|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{Nihongo|MI|彌、見、美、海、看}}.
In ancient Japanese historical texts like "[[Kojiki]]" and "[[Nihonshoki]]", people with "Mi" or "Mimi" in their names were often leaders in their regions. For instance, "{{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|en}}" ruled in the [[Izumi Province|Izumi]] and [[Settsu Province|Settsu regions]], "Kugamimi" ruled in the [[Tamba Province|Tamba region]], and "Masakitsumimi" ruled in the [[Tajima Province|Tajima region]].<ref name=":2">溝口睦子「記紀神話解釈の一つのこころみ」『文学』1973rd year - 1974th year 。波多都美は[[Izumo Province ]][[飯石郡]][[波多郷]]の首長、支自麻都美はIzumo Province 飯石郡[[来島郷]]の首長である。</ref> and minor chiefs named Hatatsumi and Kijimatsumi are mentioned.<ref name=":2" />
In ancient Japan, birds were believed to carry human souls, and catching them was a religious ritual, so the name is thought to mean "catching birds" <ref>[[西宮一民]]「付録 神名の釈義」『新潮日本古典集成 古事記』[[新潮社]]、2014th year 、381頁。</ref> .
==Other boilerplate== {{ill|Tottori-no-Kami|ja}} is one of the {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. This is a group of Japanese deities ({{ill|Kunitsukami|en}}) that are all in a genealogical relationship descending from {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Overview of the Generations == {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}} married {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (daughter of [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi]]) and had {{ill|Yashimajinumi|ja|八島士奴美神|1=Yashimajinumi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="auto">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Yashimajinumi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=181|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>.
This god married {{ill|Konohanachiru-hime|ja|木花知流比売|en}}<ref name="auto" /> (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and had {{ill|Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and had {{ill|Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|1=Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|1=Ame-no-Tsudoechine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and had {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|1=Omizunu|simple|User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Funodomuno Kami|ja|布怒豆怒神|1=Funozuno|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funodomuno Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and had {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|1=Ame-no-Fuyukinu|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and had {{ill|Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref>{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and had {{ill|Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|1=Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Ame-no-Tsudoechine|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|1=Ame-no-Tsudoechine|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and had {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|1=Omizunu|simple|User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|1=Funozuno|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and had {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|1=Ame-no-Fuyukinu|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (This Amanofuyuorihime Kami is also known as Amanofukine Kami{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}, and is said to be the child of Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The child {{ill|Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|ja|清武豊彦命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is the ancestor of the {{ill|Ono Family (Shake)|lt=Ono Family|ja|小野氏 (社家)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ono Family (Shake)|lt=Ono Family|qq=draft|qq=draft}} who were priests of {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}.)
This god married {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|1=Sashikuni Wakahime|2=ja|3=刺国若比売|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=draft|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Ōkami|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and had {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Ōnamuchi no Kami. Half-sibling to the {{ill|Yasogami|ja|八十神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}).
This god married {{ill|Tottori Kami|ja|鳥取神|1=Tottori-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tottori Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Yashimamochinogami|ja|八島牟遅能神|1=Yashimamuji|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and had {{ill|Torinarumi Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and had {{ill|Kunishirotomi Kami|ja|国忍富神|1=Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|1=Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kunishirotomi Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Ashinataka-no-Kami|ja|葦那陀迦神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and had {{ill|Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|ja|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|1=Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Sakitamahime|ja|前玉比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sakitamahime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanomikemunushi no Kami|ja|天之甕主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanomikemunushi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and had {{ill|Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|ja|甕主日子神|1=Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and had {{ill|Tahirikishimarumi Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|10=zh|11=多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tahirikishimarumi Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|ja|比比羅木之其花麻豆美神|1=Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and had {{ill|Mironami Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Aonuma-Mashi-no-Maou-hime|ja|青沼馬沼押比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Aonuma-Mashi-no-Maou-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Shikiyamanushi no Kami|ja|敷山主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shikiyamanushi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and had {{ill|Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|ja|布忍富鳥鳴海神|1=Nunobutomi-Torinarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Wakatsukime Kami|ja|若尽女神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Wakatsukime Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and had {{ill|Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|1=Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Totsumatone Kami|ja|遠津待根神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsumatone Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanosagiri Kami|ja|天之狭霧神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanosagiri Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and had {{ill|Totsuyami-Sakitara-no-Kami|ja|遠津山岬多良斯神|1=Totsuyami-Sakitara-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsuyami-Sakitara-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.

<!--Tottori-no-Kami--> <!--Tottori-no-Kami--> {{translated page|ja|鳥取神|version=101649062|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{infobox deity| member_of= {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | type = Shinto | children = | father =[[Susanoo|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] | consort = | mother = }}
Torimimi was a wife of [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]] and a member of the {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
People with mimi in their names include {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|lt=Suga no Yatsumimi|en:draft||ja||zh}}, {{ill|Futimi no Kami|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Futimi no Kami|fr|Futemimi|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Torimimi|ja|鳥取神}}, {{ill|Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|10=zh|11=多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who appear in the Izumo myth of the [[Kojiki]]. {{ill|Amenohibara Oshinomi no Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is also considered a regional chief

[[sn:Tottori-no-Kami]] [[ja:鳥取神]]
<!--Tottori-no-Kami--><!--Tottori-no-Kami-->

{{infobox deity| member_of= {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | type = Shinto | children = | father =[[Susanoo|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] | consort = | mother = }}
Torimimi was a wife of [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]] and a member of the {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
People with mimi in their names include {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|lt=Suga no Yatsumimi|en:draft||ja||zh}}, {{ill|Futimi no Kami|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Futimi no Kami|fr|Futemimi|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Torimimi|ja|鳥取神}}, {{ill|Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|10=zh|11=多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who appear in the Izumo myth of the [[Kojiki]]. {{ill|Amenohibara Oshinomi no Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is also considered a regional chief
==Overview==
She is a {{ill|Kunitsukami|en|4=fr}} that appears only in [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]] . She is the daughter of {{ill|Yashimamochinogami|ja|八島牟遅能神|1=Yashimamuji|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the sixth wife of [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Mikoto]] . In addition to Tottori-no-kami, the name is also written as '''Torimimi (鳥耳神)''' and '''Torikai no Kaim (鳥甘神)'''.<ref>『古事記伝』による。須賀之八耳は[[須賀郷]]ないしは[[須佐郷]]、[[美呂浪神]]は[[美呂郷]]、[[布忍富鳥鳴海神]]は[[布師郷]]のミ（首長）を表す。</ref>
“'''Mimi'''" and "'''Mi'''" are Japanese words that can be found in many names related to ancient Japan. They are very common in names of royal family members.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=魏志倭人伝、彌彌、彌彌那利|url=https://tokyox.sakura.ne.jp/forum/discussion/49/%E9%AD%8F%E5%BF%97%E5%80%AD%E4%BA%BA%E4%BC%9D-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C%E9%82%A3%E5%88%A9|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230126011011/https://tokyox.sakura.ne.jp/forum/discussion/49/%E9%AD%8F%E5%BF%97%E5%80%AD%E4%BA%BA%E4%BC%9D-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C%E9%82%A3%E5%88%A9|archive-date=2023-01-26|access-date=2023-01-26|website=Forum_tokyoblog}}</ref> They're also found in names of leaders, people, and objects. "Mi" can mean "divine spirit" and can be a part of names for deities. For example, the terms "[[Watatsumi]]" and "{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|en}}" mean "sea spirit" and "mountain spirit" respectively and both use the version 霊.<ref name=":1">[[本居宣長]]『[[古事記伝]]』七之巻。</ref>
Spellings include {{nihongo|Mimi|ミミ、耳、彌彌、美美|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{Nihongo|MI|彌、見、美、海、看}}.
In ancient Japanese historical texts like "[[Kojiki]]" and "[[Nihonshoki]]", people with "Mi" or "Mimi" in their names were often leaders in their regions. For instance, "{{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|en}}" ruled in the [[Izumi Province|Izumi]] and [[Settsu Province|Settsu regions]], "Kugamimi" ruled in the [[Tamba Province|Tamba region]], and "Masakitsumimi" ruled in the [[Tajima Province|Tajima region]].<ref name=":2">溝口睦子「記紀神話解釈の一つのこころみ」『文学』1973rd year - 1974th year 。波多都美は[[Izumo Province ]][[飯石郡]][[波多郷]]の首長、支自麻都美はIzumo Province 飯石郡[[来島郷]]の首長である。</ref> and minor chiefs named Hatatsumi and Kijimatsumi are mentioned.<ref name=":2" />
In ancient Japan, birds were believed to carry human souls, and catching them was a religious ritual, so the name is thought to mean "catching birds" <ref>[[西宮一民]]「付録 神名の釈義」『新潮日本古典集成 古事記』[[新潮社]]、2014th year 、381頁。</ref> .
==Other boilerplate== {{ill|Tottori-no-Kami|ja}} is one of the {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. This is a group of Japanese deities ({{ill|Kunitsukami|en}}) that are all in a genealogical relationship descending from {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.

This god married {{ill|Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and had {{ill|Kunishirotomi Kami|ja|国忍富神|1=Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|1=Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kunishirotomi Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Ashinataka-no-Kami|ja|葦那陀迦神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and had {{ill|Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|ja|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|1=Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Sakitamahime|ja|前玉比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sakitamahime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanomikemunushi no Kami|ja|天之甕主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanomikemunushi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and had {{ill|Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|ja|甕主日子神|1=Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and had {{ill|Tahirikishimarumi Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|10=zh|11=多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tahirikishimarumi Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|ja|比比羅木之其花麻豆美神|1=Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and had {{ill|Mironami Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Aonuma-Mashi-no-Maou-hime|ja|青沼馬沼押比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Aonuma-Mashi-no-Maou-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Shikiyamanushi no Kami|ja|敷山主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shikiyamanushi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and had {{ill|Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|ja|布忍富鳥鳴海神|1=Nunobutomi-Torinarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Wakatsukime Kami|ja|若尽女神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Wakatsukime Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and had {{ill|Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|1=Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This god married {{ill|Totsumatone Kami|ja|遠津待根神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsumatone Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanosagiri Kami|ja|天之狭霧神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanosagiri Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and had {{ill|Totsuyami-Sakitara-no-Kami|ja|遠津山岬多良斯神|1=Totsuyami-Sakitara-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsuyami-Sakitara-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
