<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鳥取神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鳥取神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鳥取神]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|spouse={{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}}|children={{ill|Torinarumi no kami|WD=Q55522639}}|father={{ill|Q65249018|WD=Q65249018}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q48760952]] Wikidata name: Totori no kami [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox deity| member_of= {{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|lt=The 17 Generations of Deities|lt=17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|qq=}} | type = Shinto | children = | father =[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] | consort =; {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}}| mother = | gender = {{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}} | parents = {{ill|Q65249018|WD=Q65249018}} | offspring = {{ill|Torinarumi no kami|WD=Q55522639}} }}
Torimimi was a wife of [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]] and a member of the [[Category:17_spirits_of_the_world]]{{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|lt=The 17 Generations of Deities|lt=17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|qq=}}
People with mimi in their names include {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|lt=Suga no Yatsumimi|en:draft|ja|zh|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ZH}}, {{ill|Futimi no Kami|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Futimi no Kami|fr|Futemimi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futimi no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|qq=}}, {{ill|Tottori-no-Kami|lt=Tottori-no-Kami|lt=Torimimi|ja|鳥取神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torimimi|qq=}}, {{ill|Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|10=zh|11=多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|qq=}}, who appear in the Izumo myth of the [[Kojiki]]. {{ill|Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohibara Oshinomi no Kami|qq=}} is also considered a regional chief
==Overview==
She is a {{ill|Kunitsukami|en|4=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|qq=}} that appears only in [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]] . She is the daughter of {{ill|Yashimamochinogami|ja|八島牟遅能神|1=Yashimamuji|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashimamuji|qq=}} and the sixth wife of [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Mikoto]] . In addition to Tottori-no-kami, the name is also written as '''Torimimi (鳥耳神)''' and '''Torikai no Kaim (鳥甘神)'''.<ref>『古事記伝』による。須賀之八耳は[[須賀郷]]ないしは[[須佐郷]]、[[美呂浪神]]は[[美呂郷]]、[[布忍富鳥鳴海神]]は[[布師郷]]のミ（首長）を表す。</ref>
“'''Mimi'''" and "'''Mi'''" are Japanese words that can be found in many names related to ancient Japan. They are very common in names of royal family members.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=魏志倭人伝、彌彌、彌彌那利|url=https://tokyox.sakura.ne.jp/forum/discussion/49/%E9%AD%8F%E5%BF%97%E5%80%AD%E4%BA%BA%E4%BC%9D-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C%E9%82%A3%E5%88%A9|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230126011011/https://tokyox.sakura.ne.jp/forum/discussion/49/%E9%AD%8F%E5%BF%97%E5%80%AD%E4%BA%BA%E4%BC%9D-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C%E9%82%A3%E5%88%A9|archive-date=2023-01-26|access-date=2023-01-26|website=Forum_tokyoblog}}</ref> They're also found in names of leaders, people, and objects. "Mi" can mean "divine spirit" and can be a part of names for deities. For example, the terms "[[:en:Watatsumi|Watatsumi]]" and "{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}}" mean "sea spirit" and "mountain spirit" respectively and both use the version 霊.<ref name=":1">[[:en:本居宣長|本居宣長]]『[[古事記伝]]』七之巻。</ref>
Spellings include {{nihongo|Mimi|ミミ、耳、彌彌、美美|qq=draft}} and {{Nihongo|MI|彌、見、美、海、看}}.
In ancient Japanese historical texts like "[[Kojiki]]" and "[[Nihon Shoki|Nihonshoki]]", people with "Mi" or "Mimi" in their names were often leaders in their regions. For instance, "{{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|qq=}}" ruled in the [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi]] and [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu regions]], "Kugamimi" ruled in the [[:en:Tanba Province|Tanba region]], and "Masakitsumimi" ruled in the [[:en:Tajima Province|Tajima region]].<ref name=":2">溝口睦子「記紀神話解釈の一つのこころみ」『文学』1973rd year - 1974th year 。波多都美は[[Izumo Province ]][[飯石郡]][[波多郷]]の首長、支自麻都美はIzumo Province 飯石郡[[来島郷]]の首長である。</ref> and minor chiefs named Hatatsumi and Kijimatsumi are mentioned.<ref name=":2" />
In ancient Japan, birds were believed to carry human souls, and catching them was a religious ritual, so the name is thought to mean "catching birds" <ref>[[西宮一民]]「付録 神名の釈義」『新潮日本古典集成 古事記』[[:en:新潮社|新潮社]]、2014th year 、381頁。</ref> .
==Other boilerplate== {{ill|Tottori-no-Kami|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is one of the [[Category:17_spirits_of_the_world]]{{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|lt=The 17 Generations of Deities|lt=17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|qq=}}. This is a group of Japanese deities ({{ill|Kunitsukami|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|qq=}}) that are all in a genealogical relationship descending from {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}.
== Overview of the Generations == {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} married {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}} (daughter of [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi]]) and had [[:en:Yashimajinumi|Yashimajinumi]]<ref name="auto">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Yashimajinumi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=181|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>.
This god married [[:en:Konohanachiru-hime|Konohanachiru-hime]]<ref name="auto" /> (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}) and had {{ill|Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|qq=}}.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|qq=}} (daughter of [[:en:Kuraokami|Kuraokami]]) and had {{ill|Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|1=Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|qq=}}.
This god married {{ill|Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|1=Ame-no-Tsudoechine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine|qq=}} and had [[:en:Omizunu|Omizunu]].
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|qq=}} (daughter of {{ill|Funodomuno Kami|ja|布怒豆怒神|1=Funozuno|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funodomuno Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funodomuno Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|qq=}}) and had [[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]]<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}) and had {{ill|Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|qq=}}<ref>{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|qq=}} (daughter of [[:en:Kuraokami|Kuraokami]]) and had {{ill|Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|1=Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|qq=}}.
This god married {{ill|Ame-no-Tsudoechine|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|1=Ame-no-Tsudoechine|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine|qq=}} and had [[:en:Omizunu|Omizunu]].
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|qq=}} (daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|1=Funozuno|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|qq=}}) and had [[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]]<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (This Amanofuyuorihime Kami is also known as Amanofukine Kami{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}, and is said to be the child of Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinomisaki Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The child {{ill|Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|ja|清武豊彦命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491392}} is the ancestor of the {{ill|Ono Family (Shake)|lt=Ono Family|ja|小野氏 (社家)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ono Family (Shake)|lt=Ono Family|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Family (Shake)|qq=}} who were priests of {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinomisaki Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}.)
This god married {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|1=Sashikuni Wakahime|2=ja|3=刺国若比売|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|en:draft|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=}} (daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Ōkami|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Ōkami|qq=}}) and had {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi no Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} (Ōnamuchi no Kami. Half-sibling to the {{ill|Yasogami|ja|八十神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=}}).
This god married {{ill|Tottori Kami|ja|鳥取神|1=Tottori-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tottori Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori-no-Kami|qq=}} (daughter of {{ill|Yashimamochinogami|ja|八島牟遅能神|1=Yashimamuji|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashimamuji|qq=}}) and had {{ill|Torinarumi Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi-no-Kami|qq=}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|qq=}} and had {{ill|Kunishirotomi Kami|ja|国忍富神|1=Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|1=Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kunishirotomi Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunishirotomi Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|qq=}}.
This god married {{ill|Ashinataka-no-Kami|ja|葦那陀迦神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka-no-Kami|qq=}} and had {{ill|Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|ja|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|1=Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
This god married {{ill|Sakitamahime|ja|前玉比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sakitamahime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakitamahime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanomikemunushi-no-Kami|ja|天之甕主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanomikemunushi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanomikemunushi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) and had {{ill|Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|ja|甕主日子神|1=Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (daughter of [[:en:Kuraokami|Kuraokami]]) and had {{ill|Tahirikishimarumi Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|10=zh|11=多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tahirikishimarumi Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tahirikishimarumi Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|qq=}}.
This god married {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime-no-Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (daughter of {{ill|Hihiraginosonohanamadomi-no-Kami|ja|比比羅木之其花麻豆美神|1=Hihiraginosonohanamadomi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) and had {{ill|Mironami-no-Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mironami-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
This god married {{ill|Aonuma-Mashi-no-Maou-hime|ja|青沼馬沼押比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Aonuma-Mashi-no-Maou-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aonuma-Mashi-no-Maou-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (daughter of {{ill|Shikiyamanushi-no-Kami|ja|敷山主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shikiyamanushi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikiyamanushi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) and had {{ill|Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|ja|布忍富鳥鳴海神|1=Nunobutomi-Torinarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunobutomi-Torinarumi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
This god married {{ill|Wakatsukime Kami|ja|若尽女神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Wakatsukime Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakatsukime Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and had {{ill|Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|1=Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|qq=}}.
This god married {{ill|Totsumatone-no-Kami|ja|遠津待根神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsumatone-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Totsumatone-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanosagiri-no-Kami|ja|天之狭霧神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanosagiri-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanosagiri-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}) and had {{ill|Totsuyami-Sakitara-no-Kami|ja|遠津山岬多良斯神|1=Totsuyami-Sakitara-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsuyami-Sakitara-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Totsuyami-Sakitara-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Totsuyami-Sakitara-no-Kami|qq=}}.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鳥取神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鳥取神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鳥取神]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|spouse={{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}}|children={{ill|Torinarumi no kami|WD=Q55522639}}|father={{ill|Q65249018|WD=Q65249018}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q48760952]] Wikidata name: Totori no kami [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox deity| member_of= {{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|lt=The 17 Generations of Deities|lt=17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|qq=}} | type = Shinto | children = | father =[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] | consort =; {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}}| mother = | gender = {{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}} | parents = {{ill|Q65249018|WD=Q65249018}} | offspring = {{ill|Torinarumi no kami|WD=Q55522639}} }}
Torimimi was a wife of [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]] and a member of the {{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|lt=The 17 Generations of Deities|lt=17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|qq=}}
People with mimi in their names include {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|lt=Suga no Yatsumimi|en:draft|ja|zh|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ZH}}, {{ill|Futimi no Kami|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Futimi no Kami|fr|Futemimi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futimi no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|qq=}}, {{ill|Tottori-no-Kami|lt=Tottori-no-Kami|lt=Torimimi|ja|鳥取神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torimimi|qq=}}, {{ill|Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|10=zh|11=多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|qq=}}, who appear in the Izumo myth of the [[Kojiki]]. {{ill|Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohibara Oshinomi no Kami|qq=}} is also considered a regional chief
==Overview==
She is a {{ill|Kunitsukami|en|4=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|qq=}} that appears only in [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]] . She is the daughter of {{ill|Yashimamochinogami|ja|八島牟遅能神|1=Yashimamuji|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashimamuji|qq=}} and the sixth wife of [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Mikoto]] . In addition to Tottori-no-kami, the name is also written as '''Torimimi (鳥耳神)''' and '''Torikai no Kaim (鳥甘神)'''.<ref>『古事記伝』による。須賀之八耳は[[須賀郷]]ないしは[[須佐郷]]、[[美呂浪神]]は[[美呂郷]]、[[布忍富鳥鳴海神]]は[[布師郷]]のミ（首長）を表す。</ref>
“'''Mimi'''" and "'''Mi'''" are Japanese words that can be found in many names related to ancient Japan. They are very common in names of royal family members.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=魏志倭人伝、彌彌、彌彌那利|url=https://tokyox.sakura.ne.jp/forum/discussion/49/%E9%AD%8F%E5%BF%97%E5%80%AD%E4%BA%BA%E4%BC%9D-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C%E9%82%A3%E5%88%A9|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230126011011/https://tokyox.sakura.ne.jp/forum/discussion/49/%E9%AD%8F%E5%BF%97%E5%80%AD%E4%BA%BA%E4%BC%9D-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C%E9%82%A3%E5%88%A9|archive-date=2023-01-26|access-date=2023-01-26|website=Forum_tokyoblog}}</ref> They're also found in names of leaders, people, and objects. "Mi" can mean "divine spirit" and can be a part of names for deities. For example, the terms "[[:en:Watatsumi|Watatsumi]]" and "{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}}" mean "sea spirit" and "mountain spirit" respectively and both use the version 霊.<ref name=":1">[[:en:本居宣長|本居宣長]]『[[古事記伝]]』七之巻。</ref>
Spellings include {{nihongo|Mimi|ミミ、耳、彌彌、美美|qq=draft}} and {{Nihongo|MI|彌、見、美、海、看}}.
In ancient Japanese historical texts like "[[Kojiki]]" and "[[Nihon Shoki|Nihonshoki]]", people with "Mi" or "Mimi" in their names were often leaders in their regions. For instance, "{{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|qq=}}" ruled in the [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi]] and [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu regions]], "Kugamimi" ruled in the [[:en:Tanba Province|Tanba region]], and "Masakitsumimi" ruled in the [[:en:Tajima Province|Tajima region]].<ref name=":2">溝口睦子「記紀神話解釈の一つのこころみ」『文学』1973rd year - 1974th year 。波多都美は[[Izumo Province ]][[飯石郡]][[波多郷]]の首長、支自麻都美はIzumo Province 飯石郡[[来島郷]]の首長である。</ref> and minor chiefs named Hatatsumi and Kijimatsumi are mentioned.<ref name=":2" />
In ancient Japan, birds were believed to carry human souls, and catching them was a religious ritual, so the name is thought to mean "catching birds" <ref>[[西宮一民]]「付録 神名の釈義」『新潮日本古典集成 古事記』[[:en:新潮社|新潮社]]、2014th year 、381頁。</ref> .
==Other boilerplate== {{ill|Tottori-no-Kami|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is one of the {{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|lt=The 17 Generations of Deities|lt=17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|qq=}}. This is a group of Japanese deities ({{ill|Kunitsukami|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|qq=}}) that are all in a genealogical relationship descending from {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}.
== Overview of the Generations == {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} married {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}} (daughter of [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi]]) and had [[:en:Yashimajinumi|Yashimajinumi]]<ref name="auto">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Yashimajinumi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=181|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>.
This god married [[:en:Konohanachiru-hime|Konohanachiru-hime]]<ref name="auto" /> (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}) and had {{ill|Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|qq=}}.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|qq=}} (daughter of [[:en:Kuraokami|Kuraokami]]) and had {{ill|Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|1=Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|qq=}}.
This god married {{ill|Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|1=Ame-no-Tsudoechine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine|qq=}} and had [[:en:Omizunu|Omizunu]].
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|qq=}} (daughter of {{ill|Funodomuno Kami|ja|布怒豆怒神|1=Funozuno|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funodomuno Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funodomuno Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|qq=}}) and had [[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]]<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}) and had {{ill|Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|qq=}}<ref>{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|qq=}} (daughter of [[:en:Kuraokami|Kuraokami]]) and had {{ill|Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|1=Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|qq=}}.
This god married {{ill|Ame-no-Tsudoechine|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|1=Ame-no-Tsudoechine|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine|qq=}} and had [[:en:Omizunu|Omizunu]].
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|qq=}} (daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|1=Funozuno|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|qq=}}) and had [[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]]<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (This Amanofuyuorihime Kami is also known as Amanofukine Kami{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}, and is said to be the child of Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinomisaki Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The child {{ill|Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|ja|清武豊彦命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491392}} is the ancestor of the {{ill|Ono Family (Shake)|lt=Ono Family|ja|小野氏 (社家)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ono Family (Shake)|lt=Ono Family|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Family (Shake)|qq=}} who were priests of {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinomisaki Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}.)
This god married {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|1=Sashikuni Wakahime|2=ja|3=刺国若比売|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|en:draft|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=}} (daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Ōkami|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Ōkami|qq=}}) and had {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi no Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} (Ōnamuchi no Kami. Half-sibling to the {{ill|Yasogami|ja|八十神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=}}).
This god married {{ill|Tottori Kami|ja|鳥取神|1=Tottori-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tottori Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori-no-Kami|qq=}} (daughter of {{ill|Yashimamochinogami|ja|八島牟遅能神|1=Yashimamuji|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashimamuji|qq=}}) and had {{ill|Torinarumi Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi-no-Kami|qq=}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|qq=}} and had {{ill|Kunishirotomi Kami|ja|国忍富神|1=Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|1=Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kunishirotomi Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunishirotomi Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|qq=}}.

This god married {{ill|Sakitamahime|ja|前玉比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sakitamahime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakitamahime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanomikemunushi-no-Kami|ja|天之甕主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanomikemunushi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanomikemunushi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) and had {{ill|Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|ja|甕主日子神|1=Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (daughter of [[:en:Kuraokami|Kuraokami]]) and had {{ill|Tahirikishimarumi Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|10=zh|11=多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tahirikishimarumi Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tahirikishimarumi Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|qq=}}.

This god married {{ill|Aonuma-Mashi-no-Maou-hime|ja|青沼馬沼押比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Aonuma-Mashi-no-Maou-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aonuma-Mashi-no-Maou-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (daughter of {{ill|Shikiyamanushi-no-Kami|ja|敷山主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shikiyamanushi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikiyamanushi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) and had {{ill|Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|ja|布忍富鳥鳴海神|1=Nunobutomi-Torinarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunobutomi-Torinarumi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
This god married {{ill|Wakatsukime Kami|ja|若尽女神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Wakatsukime Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakatsukime Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and had {{ill|Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|1=Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|qq=}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鳥取神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鳥取神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鳥取神]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|spouse={{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}}|children={{ill|Torinarumi no kami|WD=Q55522639}}|father={{ill|Q65249018|WD=Q65249018}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q48760952]] Wikidata name: Totori no kami [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox deity| member_of= {{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|lt=The 17 Generations of Deities|lt=17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|qq=}} | type = Shinto | children = | father =[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] | consort =; {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}}| mother = | gender = {{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}} | parents = {{ill|Q65249018|WD=Q65249018}} | offspring = {{ill|Torinarumi no kami|WD=Q55522639}} }}
Torimimi was a wife of [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]] and a member of the {{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|lt=The 17 Generations of Deities|lt=17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|qq=}}
People with mimi in their names include {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|lt=Suga no Yatsumimi|en:draft|ja|zh|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ZH}}, {{ill|Futimi no Kami|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Futimi no Kami|fr|Futemimi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futimi no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|qq=}}, {{ill|Tottori-no-Kami|lt=Tottori-no-Kami|lt=Torimimi|ja|鳥取神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torimimi|qq=}}, {{ill|Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|10=zh|11=多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|qq=}}, who appear in the Izumo myth of the [[Kojiki]]. {{ill|Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohibara Oshinomi no Kami|qq=}} is also considered a regional chief
==Overview==
She is a {{ill|Kunitsukami|en|4=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|qq=}} that appears only in [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]] . She is the daughter of {{ill|Yashimamochinogami|ja|八島牟遅能神|1=Yashimamuji|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashimamuji|qq=}} and the sixth wife of [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Mikoto]] . In addition to Tottori-no-kami, the name is also written as '''Torimimi (鳥耳神)''' and '''Torikai no Kaim (鳥甘神)'''.<ref>『古事記伝』による。須賀之八耳は[[須賀郷]]ないしは[[須佐郷]]、[[美呂浪神]]は[[美呂郷]]、[[布忍富鳥鳴海神]]は[[布師郷]]のミ（首長）を表す。</ref>
“'''Mimi'''" and "'''Mi'''" are Japanese words that can be found in many names related to ancient Japan. They are very common in names of royal family members.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=魏志倭人伝、彌彌、彌彌那利|url=https://tokyox.sakura.ne.jp/forum/discussion/49/%E9%AD%8F%E5%BF%97%E5%80%AD%E4%BA%BA%E4%BC%9D-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C%E9%82%A3%E5%88%A9|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230126011011/https://tokyox.sakura.ne.jp/forum/discussion/49/%E9%AD%8F%E5%BF%97%E5%80%AD%E4%BA%BA%E4%BC%9D-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C%E9%82%A3%E5%88%A9|archive-date=2023-01-26|access-date=2023-01-26|website=Forum_tokyoblog}}</ref> They're also found in names of leaders, people, and objects. "Mi" can mean "divine spirit" and can be a part of names for deities. For example, the terms "[[:en:Watatsumi|Watatsumi]]" and "{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}}" mean "sea spirit" and "mountain spirit" respectively and both use the version 霊.<ref name=":1">[[:en:本居宣長|本居宣長]]『[[古事記伝]]』七之巻。</ref>
Spellings include {{nihongo|Mimi|ミミ、耳、彌彌、美美|qq=draft}} and {{Nihongo|MI|彌、見、美、海、看}}.
In ancient Japanese historical texts like "[[Kojiki]]" and "[[Nihon Shoki|Nihonshoki]]", people with "Mi" or "Mimi" in their names were often leaders in their regions. For instance, "{{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|qq=}}" ruled in the [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi]] and [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu regions]], "Kugamimi" ruled in the [[:en:Tanba Province|Tanba region]], and "Masakitsumimi" ruled in the [[:en:Tajima Province|Tajima region]].<ref name=":2">溝口睦子「記紀神話解釈の一つのこころみ」『文学』1973rd year - 1974th year 。波多都美は[[Izumo Province ]][[飯石郡]][[波多郷]]の首長、支自麻都美はIzumo Province 飯石郡[[来島郷]]の首長である。</ref> and minor chiefs named Hatatsumi and Kijimatsumi are mentioned.<ref name=":2" />
In ancient Japan, birds were believed to carry human souls, and catching them was a religious ritual, so the name is thought to mean "catching birds" <ref>[[西宮一民]]「付録 神名の釈義」『新潮日本古典集成 古事記』[[:en:新潮社|新潮社]]、2014th year 、381頁。</ref> .
==Other boilerplate== {{ill|Tottori-no-Kami|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is one of the {{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|lt=The 17 Generations of Deities|lt=17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|qq=}}. This is a group of Japanese deities ({{ill|Kunitsukami|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|qq=}}) that are all in a genealogical relationship descending from {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}.
== Overview of the Generations == {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} married {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}} (daughter of [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi]]) and had [[:en:Yashimajinumi|Yashimajinumi]]<ref name="auto">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Yashimajinumi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=181|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>.
This god married [[:en:Konohanachiru-hime|Konohanachiru-hime]]<ref name="auto" /> (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}) and had {{ill|Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|qq=}}.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|qq=}} (daughter of [[:en:Kuraokami|Kuraokami]]) and had {{ill|Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|1=Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|qq=}}.
This god married {{ill|Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|1=Ame-no-Tsudoechine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine|qq=}} and had [[:en:Omizunu|Omizunu]].
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|qq=}} (daughter of {{ill|Funodomuno Kami|ja|布怒豆怒神|1=Funozuno|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funodomuno Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funodomuno Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|qq=}}) and had [[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]]<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}) and had {{ill|Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|qq=}}<ref>{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|qq=}} (daughter of [[:en:Kuraokami|Kuraokami]]) and had {{ill|Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|1=Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|qq=}}.
This god married {{ill|Ame-no-Tsudoechine|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|1=Ame-no-Tsudoechine|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine|qq=}} and had [[:en:Omizunu|Omizunu]].
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|qq=}} (daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|1=Funozuno|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|qq=}}) and had [[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]]<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (This Amanofuyuorihime Kami is also known as Amanofukine Kami{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}, and is said to be the child of Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinomisaki Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The child {{ill|Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|ja|清武豊彦命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491392}} is the ancestor of the {{ill|Ono Family (Shake)|lt=Ono Family|ja|小野氏 (社家)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ono Family (Shake)|lt=Ono Family|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Family (Shake)|qq=}} who were priests of {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinomisaki Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}.)
This god married {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|1=Sashikuni Wakahime|2=ja|3=刺国若比売|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|en:draft|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=}} (daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Ōkami|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Ōkami|qq=}}) and had {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi no Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} (Ōnamuchi no Kami. Half-sibling to the {{ill|Yasogami|ja|八十神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=}}).
This god married {{ill|Tottori Kami|ja|鳥取神|1=Tottori-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tottori Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori-no-Kami|qq=}} (daughter of {{ill|Yashimamochinogami|ja|八島牟遅能神|1=Yashimamuji|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashimamuji|qq=}}) and had {{ill|Torinarumi Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi-no-Kami|qq=}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|qq=}} and had {{ill|Kunishirotomi Kami|ja|国忍富神|1=Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|1=Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kunishirotomi Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunishirotomi Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|qq=}}.

This god married {{ill|Sakitamahime|ja|前玉比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sakitamahime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakitamahime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanomikemunushi-no-Kami|ja|天之甕主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanomikemunushi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanomikemunushi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) and had {{ill|Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|ja|甕主日子神|1=Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

This god married {{ill|Wakatsukime Kami|ja|若尽女神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Wakatsukime Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakatsukime Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and had {{ill|Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|1=Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|qq=}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鳥取神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鳥取神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鳥取神]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|spouse={{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}}|children={{ill|Torinarumi no kami|WD=Q55522639}}|father={{ill|Q65249018|WD=Q65249018}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q48760952]] Wikidata name: Totori no kami [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox deity| member_of= {{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|lt=The 17 Generations of Deities|lt=17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|qq=}} | type = Shinto | children = | father =[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] | consort =; {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}}| mother = | gender = {{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}} | parents = {{ill|Q65249018|WD=Q65249018}} | offspring = {{ill|Torinarumi no kami|WD=Q55522639}} }}
Torimimi was a wife of [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]] and a member of the {{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|lt=The 17 Generations of Deities|lt=17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|qq=}}
People with mimi in their names include {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|lt=Suga no Yatsumimi|en:draft|ja|zh|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ZH}}, {{ill|Futimi no Kami|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Futimi no Kami|fr|Futemimi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futimi no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|qq=}}, {{ill|Tottori-no-Kami|lt=Tottori-no-Kami|lt=Torimimi|ja|鳥取神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torimimi|qq=}}, {{ill|Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|10=zh|11=多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|qq=}}, who appear in the Izumo myth of the [[Kojiki]]. {{ill|Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohibara Oshinomi no Kami|qq=}} is also considered a regional chief
==Overview==
She is a {{ill|Kunitsukami|en|4=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|qq=}} that appears only in [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]] . She is the daughter of {{ill|Yashimamochinogami|ja|八島牟遅能神|1=Yashimamuji|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashimamuji|qq=}} and the sixth wife of [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Mikoto]] . In addition to Tottori-no-kami, the name is also written as '''Torimimi (鳥耳神)''' and '''Torikai no Kaim (鳥甘神)'''.<ref>『古事記伝』による。須賀之八耳は[[須賀郷]]ないしは[[須佐郷]]、[[美呂浪神]]は[[美呂郷]]、[[布忍富鳥鳴海神]]は[[布師郷]]のミ（首長）を表す。</ref>
“'''Mimi'''" and "'''Mi'''" are Japanese words that can be found in many names related to ancient Japan. They are very common in names of royal family members.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=魏志倭人伝、彌彌、彌彌那利|url=https://tokyox.sakura.ne.jp/forum/discussion/49/%E9%AD%8F%E5%BF%97%E5%80%AD%E4%BA%BA%E4%BC%9D-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C%E9%82%A3%E5%88%A9|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230126011011/https://tokyox.sakura.ne.jp/forum/discussion/49/%E9%AD%8F%E5%BF%97%E5%80%AD%E4%BA%BA%E4%BC%9D-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C%E9%82%A3%E5%88%A9|archive-date=2023-01-26|access-date=2023-01-26|website=Forum_tokyoblog}}</ref> They're also found in names of leaders, people, and objects. "Mi" can mean "divine spirit" and can be a part of names for deities. For example, the terms "[[:en:Watatsumi|Watatsumi]]" and "{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}}" mean "sea spirit" and "mountain spirit" respectively and both use the version 霊.<ref name=":1">[[:en:本居宣長|本居宣長]]『[[古事記伝]]』七之巻。</ref>
Spellings include {{nihongo|Mimi|ミミ、耳、彌彌、美美|qq=draft}} and {{Nihongo|MI|彌、見、美、海、看}}.
In ancient Japanese historical texts like "[[Kojiki]]" and "[[Nihon Shoki|Nihonshoki]]", people with "Mi" or "Mimi" in their names were often leaders in their regions. For instance, "{{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|qq=}}" ruled in the [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi]] and [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu regions]], "Kugamimi" ruled in the [[:en:Tanba Province|Tanba region]], and "Masakitsumimi" ruled in the [[:en:Tajima Province|Tajima region]].<ref name=":2">溝口睦子「記紀神話解釈の一つのこころみ」『文学』1973rd year - 1974th year 。波多都美は[[Izumo Province ]][[飯石郡]][[波多郷]]の首長、支自麻都美はIzumo Province 飯石郡[[来島郷]]の首長である。</ref> and minor chiefs named Hatatsumi and Kijimatsumi are mentioned.<ref name=":2" />
In ancient Japan, birds were believed to carry human souls, and catching them was a religious ritual, so the name is thought to mean "catching birds" <ref>[[西宮一民]]「付録 神名の釈義」『新潮日本古典集成 古事記』[[:en:新潮社|新潮社]]、2014th year 、381頁。</ref> .
==Other boilerplate== {{ill|Tottori-no-Kami|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is one of the {{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|lt=The 17 Generations of Deities|lt=17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|qq=}}. This is a group of Japanese deities ({{ill|Kunitsukami|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|qq=}}) that are all in a genealogical relationship descending from {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}.
== Overview of the Generations == {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} married {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}} (daughter of [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi]]) and had [[:en:Yashimajinumi|Yashimajinumi]]<ref name="auto">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Yashimajinumi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=181|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>.
This god married [[:en:Konohanachiru-hime|Konohanachiru-hime]]<ref name="auto" /> (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}) and had {{ill|Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|qq=}}.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|qq=}} (daughter of [[:en:Kuraokami|Kuraokami]]) and had {{ill|Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|1=Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|qq=}}.
This god married {{ill|Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|1=Ame-no-Tsudoechine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine|qq=}} and had [[:en:Omizunu|Omizunu]].
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|qq=}} (daughter of {{ill|Funodomuno Kami|ja|布怒豆怒神|1=Funozuno|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funodomuno Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funodomuno Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|qq=}}) and had [[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]]<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}) and had {{ill|Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|qq=}}<ref>{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|qq=}} (daughter of [[:en:Kuraokami|Kuraokami]]) and had {{ill|Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|1=Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|qq=}}.
This god married {{ill|Ame-no-Tsudoechine|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|1=Ame-no-Tsudoechine|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine|qq=}} and had [[:en:Omizunu|Omizunu]].
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|qq=}} (daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|1=Funozuno|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|qq=}}) and had [[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]]<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (This Amanofuyuorihime Kami is also known as Amanofukine Kami{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}, and is said to be the child of Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinomisaki Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The child {{ill|Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|ja|清武豊彦命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491392}} is the ancestor of the {{ill|Ono Family (Shake)|lt=Ono Family|ja|小野氏 (社家)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ono Family (Shake)|lt=Ono Family|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Family (Shake)|qq=}} who were priests of {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinomisaki Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}.)
This god married {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|1=Sashikuni Wakahime|2=ja|3=刺国若比売|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|en:draft|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=}} (daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Ōkami|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Ōkami|qq=}}) and had {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi no Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} (Ōnamuchi no Kami. Half-sibling to the {{ill|Yasogami|ja|八十神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=}}).
This god married {{ill|Tottori Kami|ja|鳥取神|1=Tottori-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tottori Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori-no-Kami|qq=}} (daughter of {{ill|Yashimamochinogami|ja|八島牟遅能神|1=Yashimamuji|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashimamuji|qq=}}) and had {{ill|Torinarumi Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi-no-Kami|qq=}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|qq=}} and had {{ill|Kunishirotomi Kami|ja|国忍富神|1=Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|1=Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kunishirotomi Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunishirotomi Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|qq=}}.
This god married {{ill|Ashinataka-no-Kami|ja|葦那陀迦神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka-no-Kami|qq=}} and had {{ill|Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|ja|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|1=Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
This god married {{ill|Sakitamahime|ja|前玉比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sakitamahime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakitamahime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanomikemunushi-no-Kami|ja|天之甕主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanomikemunushi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanomikemunushi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) and had {{ill|Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|ja|甕主日子神|1=Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (daughter of [[:en:Kuraokami|Kuraokami]]) and had {{ill|Tahirikishimarumi Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|10=zh|11=多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tahirikishimarumi Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tahirikishimarumi Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|qq=}}.
This god married {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime-no-Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (daughter of {{ill|Hihiraginosonohanamadomi-no-Kami|ja|比比羅木之其花麻豆美神|1=Hihiraginosonohanamadomi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) and had {{ill|Mironami-no-Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mironami-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
This god married {{ill|Aonuma-Mashi-no-Maou-hime|ja|青沼馬沼押比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Aonuma-Mashi-no-Maou-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aonuma-Mashi-no-Maou-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (daughter of {{ill|Shikiyamanushi-no-Kami|ja|敷山主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shikiyamanushi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikiyamanushi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) and had {{ill|Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|ja|布忍富鳥鳴海神|1=Nunobutomi-Torinarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunobutomi-Torinarumi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
This god married {{ill|Wakatsukime Kami|ja|若尽女神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Wakatsukime Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakatsukime Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and had {{ill|Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|1=Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|qq=}}.
This god married {{ill|Totsumatone-no-Kami|ja|遠津待根神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsumatone-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Totsumatone-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanosagiri-no-Kami|ja|天之狭霧神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanosagiri-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanosagiri-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}) and had {{ill|Totsuyami-Sakitara-no-Kami|ja|遠津山岬多良斯神|1=Totsuyami-Sakitara-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsuyami-Sakitara-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Totsuyami-Sakitara-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Totsuyami-Sakitara-no-Kami|qq=}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鳥取神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鳥取神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鳥取神]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|spouse={{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}}|children={{ill|Torinarumi no kami|WD=Q55522639}}|father={{ill|Q65249018|WD=Q65249018}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q48760952]] Wikidata name: Totori no kami [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox deity| member_of= {{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|lt=The 17 Generations of Deities|lt=17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|qq=}} | type = Shinto | children = | father =[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] | consort =; {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}}| mother = | gender = {{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}} | parents = {{ill|Q65249018|WD=Q65249018}} | offspring = {{ill|Torinarumi no kami|WD=Q55522639}} }}
Torimimi was a wife of [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]] and a member of the {{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|lt=The 17 Generations of Deities|lt=17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|qq=}}
People with mimi in their names include {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|lt=Suga no Yatsumimi|en:draft|ja|zh|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ZH}}, {{ill|Futimi no Kami|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Futimi no Kami|fr|Futemimi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futimi no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|qq=}}, {{ill|Tottori-no-Kami|lt=Tottori-no-Kami|lt=Torimimi|ja|鳥取神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torimimi|qq=}}, {{ill|Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|10=zh|11=多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|qq=}}, who appear in the Izumo myth of the [[Kojiki]]. {{ill|Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohibara Oshinomi no Kami|qq=}} is also considered a regional chief
==Overview==
She is a {{ill|Kunitsukami|en|4=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|qq=}} that appears only in [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]] . She is the daughter of {{ill|Yashimamochinogami|ja|八島牟遅能神|1=Yashimamuji|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashimamuji|qq=}} and the sixth wife of [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Mikoto]] . In addition to Tottori-no-kami, the name is also written as '''Torimimi (鳥耳神)''' and '''Torikai no Kaim (鳥甘神)'''.<ref>『古事記伝』による。須賀之八耳は[[須賀郷]]ないしは[[須佐郷]]、[[美呂浪神]]は[[美呂郷]]、[[布忍富鳥鳴海神]]は[[布師郷]]のミ（首長）を表す。</ref>
“'''Mimi'''" and "'''Mi'''" are Japanese words that can be found in many names related to ancient Japan. They are very common in names of royal family members.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=魏志倭人伝、彌彌、彌彌那利|url=https://tokyox.sakura.ne.jp/forum/discussion/49/%E9%AD%8F%E5%BF%97%E5%80%AD%E4%BA%BA%E4%BC%9D-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C%E9%82%A3%E5%88%A9|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230126011011/https://tokyox.sakura.ne.jp/forum/discussion/49/%E9%AD%8F%E5%BF%97%E5%80%AD%E4%BA%BA%E4%BC%9D-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C%E9%82%A3%E5%88%A9|archive-date=2023-01-26|access-date=2023-01-26|website=Forum_tokyoblog}}</ref> They're also found in names of leaders, people, and objects. "Mi" can mean "divine spirit" and can be a part of names for deities. For example, the terms "[[:en:Watatsumi|Watatsumi]]" and "{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}}" mean "sea spirit" and "mountain spirit" respectively and both use the version 霊.<ref name=":1">[[:en:本居宣長|本居宣長]]『[[古事記伝]]』七之巻。</ref>
Spellings include {{nihongo|Mimi|ミミ、耳、彌彌、美美|qq=draft}} and {{Nihongo|MI|彌、見、美、海、看}}.
In ancient Japanese historical texts like "[[Kojiki]]" and "[[Nihon Shoki|Nihonshoki]]", people with "Mi" or "Mimi" in their names were often leaders in their regions. For instance, "{{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|qq=}}" ruled in the [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi]] and [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu regions]], "Kugamimi" ruled in the [[:en:Tanba Province|Tanba region]], and "Masakitsumimi" ruled in the [[:en:Tajima Province|Tajima region]].<ref name=":2">溝口睦子「記紀神話解釈の一つのこころみ」『文学』1973rd year - 1974th year 。波多都美は[[Izumo Province ]][[飯石郡]][[波多郷]]の首長、支自麻都美はIzumo Province 飯石郡[[来島郷]]の首長である。</ref> and minor chiefs named Hatatsumi and Kijimatsumi are mentioned.<ref name=":2" />
In ancient Japan, birds were believed to carry human souls, and catching them was a religious ritual, so the name is thought to mean "catching birds" <ref>[[西宮一民]]「付録 神名の釈義」『新潮日本古典集成 古事記』[[:en:新潮社|新潮社]]、2014th year 、381頁。</ref> .
==Other boilerplate== {{ill|Tottori-no-Kami|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is one of the {{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|lt=The 17 Generations of Deities|lt=17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|qq=}}. This is a group of Japanese deities ({{ill|Kunitsukami|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|qq=}}) that are all in a genealogical relationship descending from {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}.
== Overview of the Generations == {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} married {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}} (daughter of [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi]]) and had [[:en:Yashimajinumi|Yashimajinumi]]<ref name="auto">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Yashimajinumi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=181|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>.
This god married [[:en:Konohanachiru-hime|Konohanachiru-hime]]<ref name="auto" /> (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}) and had {{ill|Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|qq=}}.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|qq=}} (daughter of [[:en:Kuraokami|Kuraokami]]) and had {{ill|Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|1=Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|qq=}}.
This god married {{ill|Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|1=Ame-no-Tsudoechine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine|qq=}} and had [[:en:Omizunu|Omizunu]].
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|qq=}} (daughter of {{ill|Funodomuno Kami|ja|布怒豆怒神|1=Funozuno|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funodomuno Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funodomuno Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|qq=}}) and had [[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]]<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}) and had {{ill|Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|qq=}}<ref>{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|qq=}} (daughter of [[:en:Kuraokami|Kuraokami]]) and had {{ill|Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|1=Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|qq=}}.
This god married {{ill|Ame-no-Tsudoechine|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|1=Ame-no-Tsudoechine|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine|qq=}} and had [[:en:Omizunu|Omizunu]].
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|qq=}} (daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|1=Funozuno|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|qq=}}) and had [[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]]<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (This Amanofuyuorihime Kami is also known as Amanofukine Kami{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}, and is said to be the child of Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinomisaki Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The child {{ill|Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|ja|清武豊彦命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491392}} is the ancestor of the {{ill|Ono Family (Shake)|lt=Ono Family|ja|小野氏 (社家)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ono Family (Shake)|lt=Ono Family|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Family (Shake)|qq=}} who were priests of {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinomisaki Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}.)
This god married {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|1=Sashikuni Wakahime|2=ja|3=刺国若比売|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|en:draft|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=}} (daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Ōkami|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Ōkami|qq=}}) and had {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi no Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} (Ōnamuchi no Kami. Half-sibling to the {{ill|Yasogami|ja|八十神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=}}).
This god married {{ill|Tottori Kami|ja|鳥取神|1=Tottori-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tottori Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori-no-Kami|qq=}} (daughter of {{ill|Yashimamochinogami|ja|八島牟遅能神|1=Yashimamuji|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashimamuji|qq=}}) and had {{ill|Torinarumi Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi-no-Kami|qq=}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|qq=}} and had {{ill|Kunishirotomi Kami|ja|国忍富神|1=Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|1=Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kunishirotomi Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunishirotomi Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|qq=}}.

This god married {{ill|Sakitamahime|ja|前玉比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sakitamahime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakitamahime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanomikemunushi-no-Kami|ja|天之甕主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanomikemunushi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanomikemunushi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) and had {{ill|Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|ja|甕主日子神|1=Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
